2605.00888.title: Selective Correlation Based Knowledge Distillation for Ground Reaction Force Estimation
2605.00888.paper_summary: Wearable sensor-based human gait analysis holds great promise in healthcare, rehabilitation, clinical diagnosis and monitoring, and sports activities. Specifically, ground reaction force (GRF) provides essential insights into the body's interaction with the ground during movement and is typically measured using instrumented treadmills equipped with force plates. However, such equipment is expensive and restricted to laboratory environments. To enable a more portable solution, wearable insole sensors have been used to measure GRF. These sensors, however, are prone to noise and external interference, which reduces measurement accuracy. Deep learning methodologies could be adopted to address these issues, but they often require significant computing resources to achieve high accuracy, limiting their applicability for real-time analysis on portable devices. To overcome these limitations, we propose Selective Correlation Based Knowledge Distillation (SCKD) for estimating GRF from data collected by insole sensors. Our proposed method utilizes selected features considering temporal characteristics in the process of extracting correlation maps for knowledge transfer, enhancing interpretability and mitigating issues in high dimensional data processing. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the compact models generated by our distillation framework through comparison with existing methods. Various configurations of teacher-student architectures and training approaches are examined based on multiple evaluation criteria, utilizing data collected at different walking speeds and with different window sizes. Experimental results confirm that our approach outperforms existing methods in estimating GRF from wearable insole sensor data. Therefore, our approach offers a reliable and resource-efficient solution for human gait analysis.
2605.00888.paper_summary_zh: 可穿戴傳感器的人類步態分析在醫療保健、康復、臨床診斷和監測以及運動活動中具有巨大的潛力。<lb/>具體而言，地面反作用力（GRF）提供了有關身體在運動過程中與地面互動的重要見解，通常使用配備有力板的儀器化跑步機進行測量。<lb/>然而，這類設備價格昂貴且僅限於實驗室環境。<lb/>為了實現更便攜的解決方案，已經使用可穿戴鞋墊傳感器來測量GRF。<lb/>然而，這些傳感器容易受到噪聲和外部干擾的影響，從而降低了測量的準確性。<lb/>深度學習方法可以被採用來解決這些問題，但它們通常需要大量的計算資源才能達到高準確度，限制了它們在便攜設備上進行實時分析的應用。<lb/>為了克服這些限制，我們提出了基於選擇性相關的知識蒸餾（SCKD）方法，用於從鞋墊傳感器收集的數據中估算GRF。<lb/>我們提出的方法利用選定的特徵，考慮到提取相關圖進行知識轉移過程中的時間特性，增強了解釋性並減輕高維數據處理中的問題。<lb/>我們通過與現有方法的比較，展示了我們的蒸餾框架生成的緊湊模型的有效性。<lb/>根據多個評估標準，檢查了各種教師-學生架構和訓練方法的配置，利用在不同步行速度和不同窗口大小下收集的數據。<lb/>實驗結果確認我們的方法在從可穿戴鞋墊傳感器數據中估算GRF方面優於現有方法。<lb/>因此，我們的方法為人類步態分析提供了一個可靠且資源高效的解決方案。
2605.00888.author: Eun Som Jeon et.al.
2605.00888.authors: Eun Som Jeon, Jisoo Lee, Huisu Lim, Omik M. Save, Hyunglae Lee, Pavan Turaga
